content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN such that:


2×(A1⊕A2⊕…⊕AN)≥(A1∣A2∣…∣AN)2\times (A_1 \oplus A_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus A_N) \geq (A_1 | A_2 | \ldots | A_N)2×(A1​⊕A2​⊕…⊕AN​)≥(A1​∣A2​∣…∣AN​), where ⊕\oplus⊕ and ∣|∣ denote the bitwise xor and or operations respectively.
Your task is to perform the following operation until the array is empty:


Choose any element AiA_iAi​ from the array.
Remove AiA_iAi​ from the array.
Perform **one** of the following:

Use a new box and add AiA_iAi​ to the box.
Select an existing box such that the XOR of all the elements in the box (before placing AiA_iAi​) is **greater than or equal to** AiA_iAi​ and add AiA_iAi​ to the box.


Determine the **minimum** number of boxes required to empty the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the number of elements in AAA.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of boxes required to empty the array AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Choose 666 from the array and delete it.

Add 666 to a new box.



AAA is empty now and we used one box.
Test case 222:

Choose 121212 from the array and delete it.

Add 121212 to a new box. The XOR of the box becomes 121212.


Choose 333 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 12≥312 \geq 312≥3, we can add 333 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 12⊕3=1512\oplus3=1512⊕3=15.


Choose 222 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 15≥215 \geq 215≥2, we can add 222 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 15⊕2=1315\oplus2=1315⊕2=13.


Choose 111 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 13≥113 \geq 113≥1, we can add 111 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 13⊕1=1213\oplus1=1213⊕1=12.



AAA is empty now and we used one box.
Test case 333:

Choose 555 from the array and delete it.

Add 555 to a new box. The XOR of the box becomes 555.


Choose 444 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 5≥45 \geq 45≥4, we can add 444 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 5⊕4=15\oplus4=15⊕4=1.


Choose 666 from the array and delete it.

Add 666 to a new box. The XOR of the second box is 666.



AAA is now empty and we used two boxes.
